name: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
context_primitives: JSON-RPC client/server protocol: tools (model-invoked actions), resources (URI-addressable read-only data), prompts (templates). Hosts spawn clients per server with user consent boundaries.
deployment_pattern: Declare MCP servers in host config (Cursor, Claude Desktop, Codex, etc.); implement servers in TS/Python/Go; expose least-privilege capabilities per integration.
benchmarks_eval_alignment: N/A protocol-level; adoption measured by ecosystem size and host support.
ecosystem_integrations: Bridges LLM hosts to filesystems, SaaS APIs, databases, browsers (via server implementations).
differentiators: Replaces N bespoke plugins per vendor with one interoperable surface for tools and resources.
significance_for_builders: Move bulky or sensitive context out of the prompt: fetch on demand via resources/tools instead of dumping logs into chat.
key_timeline_or_milestones: Anthropic announced MCP November 2024; specification evolves with dated releases on modelcontextprotocol.io.

primary_sources.title: Anthropic — Introducing the Model Context Protocol
